food_name: Starbucks Veranda Blend Blonde Roast Ground Coffee

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for Starbucks Veranda Blend; closest parent is ground roasted coffee food product (FOODON_03301339).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers roasted ground coffee generically; no brand-specific Veranda Blend code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34325006 covers coffee as a substance; no brand-specific code exists for Veranda Blend.

serving.description: 1 tablespoon ground coffee (approx. 6 g) brewed to yield ~180 ml cup
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single serving of ground coffee is approximately 6 g (one level tablespoon), brewed with about 180 ml of hot water to produce one cup of black coffee.

nutrient_source.title: Coffee, brewed from grounds, prepared with tap water — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171890)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Veranda Blend is a lightly roasted Arabica ground coffee sold as a dry product (250 g bag). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed ground coffee (FDC ID 171890), which represents the closest approved-source match for the beverage as consumed. Per-100g values reflect brewed black coffee, the standard consumption form. The dry ground coffee itself has negligible macro contribution per typical serving. This adaptation is documented because no USDA Branded Food entry for Starbucks Veranda Blend ground coffee was available with complete nutrient data at time of processing.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of black coffee — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Wolever et al.)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Black coffee (brewed, unsweetened) contains negligible glycemic carbohydrates and has an effectively zero glycemic index. The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature confirm that plain black coffee does not raise blood glucose meaningfully. Per the zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value representing effectively zero GI), gi_category is set to 'low', and glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g per serving is 0. This applies to Veranda Blend brewed black coffee as consumed.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content; caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Brewed Veranda Blend black coffee contains approximately 2 mg sodium per 100 ml and poses minimal sodium-related hypertension risk, though individuals sensitive to caffeine should limit intake to 1–2 cups per day as caffeine can transiently raise blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible glycemic carbohydrates; epidemiological evidence suggests moderate unsweetened coffee consumption may improve insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Unsweetened Veranda Blend brewed coffee contains no glycemic carbohydrates and has a glycemic load of zero per serving, making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added sugar or high-fat dairy.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; moderate coffee consumption associated with neutral to beneficial cardiovascular outcomes in epidemiological studies
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Brewed Veranda Blend black coffee contains essentially no saturated fat or cholesterol, and moderate consumption (up to 3–4 cups per day) is not contraindicated for most individuals with cardiovascular disease, though those with arrhythmias should consult their physician regarding caffeine intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Starbucks Veranda Blend is a Blonde Roast ground coffee sold in a 250 g bag, available through Starbucks Singapore outlets and field survey delivery. It is made from lightly roasted Arabica coffee beans and is characterised by a mild, smooth flavour profile with nuances of cocoa and lightly toasted nuts — hallmarks of the blonde roast style, which uses a shorter roasting time than medium or dark roasts to preserve delicate floral and nutty notes. The product is sold as pre-ground coffee suitable for home brewing via drip, pour-over, or French press methods. Custom grind options are available in-store. As a brewed black coffee, Veranda Blend is extremely low in calories (approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml), contains negligible protein, fat, and carbohydrates, and has a glycemic load of zero, making it appropriate for calorie-conscious individuals and those managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease when consumed without added sugar or cream. It provides small amounts of potassium, magnesium, and B vitamins (riboflavin, niacin) from the coffee bean. Caffeine content is typical of light roast Arabica coffee. The product is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is not a food in the traditional nutritional sense but a commercially packaged coffee product intended for beverage preparation.
common_user_queries: Is Veranda Blend a light roast or dark roast?
How many calories in Starbucks Veranda Blend coffee?
Is Veranda Blend suitable for diabetics?
What does Veranda Blend taste like?
Is Starbucks Veranda Blend available in Singapore?
How do I brew Veranda Blend at home?
What is the GI of Veranda Blend coffee?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy brewed black coffee (FDC ID 171890), which is the closest approved-source match for this product as consumed. No USDA Branded Food entry with complete nutrient data was available for Starbucks Veranda Blend ground coffee at time of processing. The product identity (Starbucks Veranda Blend Blonde Roast, 250 g) is confirmed from the field survey data source record. Confidence is medium rather than high because the nutrient data reflects a generic brewed coffee analogue rather than a direct laboratory analysis of this specific product.
notes: 1. Nutrient values represent brewed black coffee per 100 ml (as consumed), adapted from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171890. The dry ground coffee product itself would have very different per-100g macros, but the nutritionally relevant form is the brewed beverage. 2. carbohydrate_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for brewed black coffee; black coffee contains no glycemic carbohydrates. sugar_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for brewed coffee. saturated_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. selenium_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for brewed coffee (FDC ID 171890). 4. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category is low per convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g per serving is 0. 5. Multilingual local_names are null because Veranda Blend is a globally branded commercial coffee product of US origin (Starbucks), not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish requiring mandatory local-language naming under the Veritas multilingual rules. The cuisine category is 'Coffee' (not a Singaporean dish cuisine), so G6 multilingual naming compliance does not require Chinese, Malay, Tamil, or Hindi names. 6. Price from field survey data: SGD 17.70 (250 g bag). 7. Custom grind options available in-store at Starbucks Singapore. Items sold with minimum 14 days before best-before date.

improvement_actions_taken: Applied zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule: glycemic_index set to 1, gi_category set to 'low', glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0.
Documented all zero macro and micronutrient values with 'confirmed zero' justification phrases in notes referencing USDA FoodData Central.
Documented vitamin_e_mg=-1 sentinel value with explicit reason in notes.
Verified aliases_by_language english entries are verbatim present in flat aliases list.
Applied ontology honesty: all three ontology anchors use 'closest_parent' match_type with gap notes; no codes invented.
Confirmed G6 multilingual compliance: Veranda Blend is a US-origin branded coffee product under 'Coffee' cuisine, not a Singaporean dish; mandatory Chinese/Malay/Tamil/Hindi names not required.
nutrient_source adapted from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171890 (brewed black coffee) as closest approved-source match; adaptation documented.
